protocol.name: xdg_output_unstable_v1

interface.name: zxdg_output_manager_v1
interface.description: A global factory interface for xdg_output objects.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Using this request a client can tell the server that it is not going to use the xdg_output_manager object anymore.

request.name: get_xdg_output

description.summary: create an xdg output from a wl_output

request.description: Create an xdg_output object for the given wl_output.

interface.name: zxdg_output_v1
interface.description: An xdg_output object describes an output from the compositor's perspective. It provides desktop-oriented information like the output's position and size in the compositor's global coordinate space.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Using this request a client can tell the server that it is not going to use the xdg_output object anymore.

event.name: logical_position

description.summary: position of the output within the desktop

event.description: The position event describes the location of the wl_output within the overall desktop layout.

event.name: logical_size

description.summary: size of the output in the desktop layout

event.description: The size event describes the size of the wl_output in the overall desktop layout.

event.name: done

description.summary: all information about the output has been sent

event.description: This event is deprecated. In version 3, all properties are sent immediately after the xdg_output is created, and the wl_output.done event is sent after all xdg_output properties have been sent.

event.name: name
event.description: Many compositors can assign names to outputs that correspond to the connector name (e.g. "HDMI-A-1").

event.name: description
event.description: Many compositors can produce human-readable descriptions of their outputs (e.g. "Acme 27" 4K UHD Display (DP-1)").
